module.title: Technical Writing
module.focus: Write and publish a technical blog post about cybersecurity
module.output: One published blog post shared on LinkedIn and Twitter with SEO optimization

topics.title: Blog Post Writing & Publishing
topics.description: Structure a technical blog post, apply SEO basics, and publish on a free platform like Dev.to or Hashnode.
topics.content:
:::objectives
- Choose a cybersecurity topic you can explain clearly
- Structure a post with introduction, technical content, commands, screenshots, and conclusion
- Apply SEO basics for technical articles
- Publish on Dev.to, Hashnode, or Medium
- Share on LinkedIn and Twitter
:::

## Topic Selection

Choose a topic you have personally worked on:

| Good Topics | Avoid |
|---|---|
| "How I Set Up Wazuh SIEM on Ubuntu" | "What is SIEM?" (too generic) |
| "SQL Injection in DVWA: Step by Step" | "Web Security Overview" (too broad) |
| "Building a Python Port Scanner" | "Python for Security" (no specific project) |
| "Hardening SSH on a VPS" | "Server Security Tips" (no hands-on detail) |

## Post Structure

### 1. Title (SEO-optimized)

```
How I [Did Specific Thing] - A Step-by-Step Guide with [Tool/Technology]
```

### 2. Introduction (200 words)

```markdown
In this post, I will walk through how I [specific achievement]. I ran into [specific problem],
and this guide documents the exact steps I took to solve it.

What you'll learn:
- [Specific skill 1]
- [Specific skill 2]

Prerequisites:
- [What the reader needs before starting]
```

### 3. Technical Content

Include actual commands with output:

```markdown
## Step 1: Install Wazuh Agent

```bash
curl -sO https://packages.wazuh.com/4.7/wazuh-agent-4.7.0.deb
sudo dpkg -i wazuh-agent-4.7.0.deb
sudo systemctl enable wazuh-agent
sudo systemctl start wazuh-agent
```

Expected output:
```
● wazuh-agent.service - Wazuh Agent
     Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/wazuh-agent.service; enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Mon 2024-01-15 10:30:00 UTC
```
```

### 4. Screenshots (annotated)

Take screenshots of terminal commands, dashboard views, configuration files, and results.

### 5. Conclusion

```markdown
## What I Learned
- [Key takeaway 1]
- [Key takeaway 2]
- [Unexpected challenge and how I solved it]

## Resources
- [Official documentation link]
- [Helpful blog or tutorial]
```

## SEO Basics for Technical Articles

| Element | Best Practice |
|---------|---------------|
| Title | Include primary keyword, be specific (60 chars max) |
| Meta description | 150-160 characters summarizing the post |
| Headers | Use H2/H3 with keywords naturally |
| Images | Alt text describing the image content |
| Links | Link to authoritative sources |
| Tags | Use 3-5 relevant tags |

## Publishing Platforms

**Dev.to**: developer-friendly, Markdown support, built-in code highlighting, good SEO.
**Hashnode**: custom domain support, newsletter feature, developer-focused.
**Medium**: largest audience, but paywall can limit reach.

:::tip
Write what you built, not what you read. Posts about hands-on projects perform 3-5x better than theoretical summaries.
:::

:::classwork
Exercise: Write and publish a technical blog post about one of your portfolio projects. Include at least 3 code blocks, 2 screenshots, and proper SEO tags. Share the link on LinkedIn.
:::
topics.interviewQuestion: How does technical writing demonstrate your communication skills to employers?
topics.interviewAnswer: Technical writing shows you can document complex processes clearly - a critical SOC skill. Analysts must write incident reports, runbooks, and documentation that non-technical stakeholders understand. A published blog post is evidence of this ability and demonstrates initiative and passion for the field.

quiz.question: Why are hands-on project posts more effective than theoretical summaries?
options: They provide unique, practical value that readers can replicate
They rank lower in search results
They take less time to write
quiz.explanation: Practical posts with specific steps and results provide actionable value that generic summaries cannot.

quiz.question: What is the ideal title format for a technical blog post?
options: "How I [Did Specific Thing] - A Step-by-Step Guide with [Tool]"
quiz.explanation: Specific titles with actionable outcomes and tool names perform best in search.

quiz.question: Why include actual command output in a blog post?
options: It proves the steps work and helps readers verify their results
quiz.explanation: Showing expected output helps readers confirm they are on the right track.

quiz.question: How many tags should you use on a Dev.to post?
quiz.explanation: 3-5 relevant tags improve discoverability without looking spammy.

quiz.question: What should a blog post conclusion include?
options: Key takeaways, challenges faced, and links to resources
A request for likes and subscribes
Nothing - end after the technical content
quiz.explanation: Conclusions should summarize key learnings, mention challenges, and provide further reading links.

quiz.question: Why share blog posts on LinkedIn?
options: It demonstrates initiative and expertise to recruiters
quiz.explanation: Sharing technical work on LinkedIn signals professionalism and expertise to your network and recruiters.

quiz.question: What is the recommended character count for a meta description?
quiz.explanation: Search engines display approximately 150-160 characters of meta description.

quiz.question: Why is alt text important for blog post images?
options: It improves SEO and accessibility for screen readers
quiz.explanation: Alt text helps search engines understand image content and makes posts accessible.

quiz.question: Which platform is best for developer blog posts?
quiz.explanation: Dev.to and Hashnode are developer-focused platforms with Markdown support and strong SEO.

quiz.question: What makes a blog introduction effective?
options: Specific problem, what you will learn, and prerequisites
A list of every tool you have ever used
quiz.explanation: An effective intro states the specific problem, outlines what the reader will learn, and lists prerequisites.
